stories.title: Tarik Skubal Return on the Horizon — Tigers Ace "Awesome" in Simulated Game

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tigers manager Hinch on Tarik Skubal's simulated game:

"What I saw was an athlete free and pitching... not rehabbing."

The back-to-back AL Cy Young winner is targeting a big-league return in ~2 weeks.

#DetroitTigers are getting their ace back.

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal update:

✅ Nano-scope procedure (May 6)
✅ Simulated game — "awesome" per Hinch
⏳ Minor league rehab start: next week
⏳ Big-league return: ~2 weeks

The defending AL Cy Young winner is almost back. 🔥

#Tigers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Detroit Tigers fans, the news you've been waiting for is finally here. ⚾

Manager A.J. Hinch described Tarik Skubal's recent simulated game in glowing terms — "What I saw was an athlete free and pitching and competing and not rehabbing" — and said the Tigers are at "a very good step to advance him." According to reports, Skubal is expected to begin a minor league rehab assignment next week, with a projected big-league return approximately two weeks from now.

The back-to-back AL Cy Young Award winner underwent a nano-scope procedure on May 6 to remove a loose body from his throwing elbow. Before hitting the IL, he had posted a 2.70 ERA with 45 strikeouts in 7 starts. The Tigers have been struggling in his absence, and his name has even surfaced in trade speculation — a byproduct of a team in flux without its anchor.

But the ace is almost back. And Detroit needs him.

When will Skubal be at his best again? Drop your timeline prediction below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: "What I saw was an athlete free and pitching." — Manager A.J. Hinch on Tarik Skubal's simulated game. The back-to-back AL Cy Young winner is almost back. 🔥 #Tigers #MLB

article.headline: Tarik Skubal Is Almost Back — and the Tigers Need Him More Than Ever

stories.title: Aaron Judge on Pace for 5th Career 50-HR Season — Would Be MLB First

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge has 17 home runs and is on pace for 50+ in 2026.

Babe Ruth hit 50+ HRs in 4 different seasons. Nobody has ever done it 5 times.

Judge already has 4 (52, 62, 58, 53).

Is he really about to be the first ever? 🤯

#Yankees #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Judge's four 50-HR seasons: 52, 62, 58, 53.

The Babe had four 50-HR seasons. Nobody has ever had five.

Judge is on pace. It's June 3.

This is quietly one of the biggest individual stories in baseball.

#Yankees #MLB

facebook.long_form:
We may be watching baseball history in slow motion, and it barely has anyone's full attention.

Aaron Judge already has four career seasons with 50 or more home runs: 52 in 2017, 62 in 2022, 58 in 2024, and 53 in 2025. Through approximately 55 games of the 2026 season, he has 17 home runs and is on pace to reach 50 again.

Here's why that matters: No player in Major League Baseball history has ever hit 50+ home runs in five different seasons. Babe Ruth had four. Judge already has four. A fifth would be a record that stands completely alone — a milestone nobody has ever reached.

It's June. There are more than 100 games to go. But Judge has been one of the most consistent power hitters in the sport for nearly a decade. He's not fading. He's not slowing down. He is, if anything, exactly the kind of player who does what nobody has ever done before.

Keep watching.

Who's your pick to finish with more HRs in 2026 — Judge or Schwarber? 👇
facebook.image_caption: 4 seasons of 50+ HRs. On pace for a 5th. No player in MLB history has done it 5 times. Aaron Judge is quietly chasing history. 🔥 #Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Aaron Judge Is on Pace for His 5th Career 50-HR Season — No Player Has Ever Done That

This appears to be correct but marked MEDIUM pending verification.

stories.title: Schwarber vs. Judge — MLB's Most Compelling HR Race

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
MLB home run leaders entering June 3:

1. Kyle Schwarber (PHI) — 22 HRs
2. Aaron Judge (NYY) — 17 HRs

Schwarber pace: 66 HRs.
Judge pace: 50+ HRs.

Two players on historic paces at the same time. The last time this happened was 2001.

#MLB #Phillies #Yankees

facebook.long_form:
Here's a fact: Kyle Schwarber reached 20 home runs on May 15. That is the earliest date in Major League Baseball history that any player has hit 20 home runs in a season.

Here's another fact: Through Tuesday, Schwarber leads all MLB hitters with 22 home runs, five ahead of Aaron Judge (17) in second place. Schwarber is on pace for 66 for the season. Judge is on pace for 50+.

Two players on 50+ home run paces in early June. The last time MLB had that? Bonds and Sosa in 2001. Bonds finished with 73. Sosa had 64.

We're not predicting anything close to that. But these are the two most electric power hitters in the sport right now, on the same calendar, competing for the same record. The Phillies under new manager Don Mattingly are 10-3, with Schwarber as the engine. The Yankees are circling in the AL East.

This race is just getting started.

Who will finish with more home runs this season — Schwarber or Judge? 👇
facebook.image_caption: Schwarber: 22 HRs. Judge: 17 HRs. Two players on 50+ home run paces in early June. The MLB HR race is the best storyline in baseball right now. ⚾ #Phillies #Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber vs. Judge: MLB's Most Compelling Home Run Race Is Just Getting Started

stories.title: Braves Roar to 40-20 — Acuña Homers in 4 Straight Games

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Ronald Acuña Jr. has homered in 4 straight games. 5 HRs in that stretch.

The Atlanta Braves are 40-20 and lead the NL East by 9.5 games.

The rest of the NL is not catching up. 🪖

#Braves #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The Atlanta Braves are 40-20 and lead the NL East by 9.5 games. At this rate, the only question isn't whether they'll make the playoffs — it's whether they'll have the best record in all of baseball when October arrives.

The most exciting part right now? Ronald Acuña Jr.

After dealing with early-season injury issues, Acuña has been on a tear. He's homered in four consecutive games, including five home runs in that stretch. The 2023 NL MVP looks re-energized, re-healthy, and re-dangerous. When Acuña is locked in like this, the Braves are terrifying — and they were already terrifying.

Atlanta's combination of pitching depth, lineup balance, and now a fully healthy Acuña makes them the clear favorite to represent the National League in October. It's only June, but this team looks different from the rest of the field.

Do you think the Braves run away with the NL? Let us know! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Ronald Acuña Jr.: 4 straight games with a home run. 5 HRs in that stretch. The Braves are 40-20 and the rest of the NL is not keeping up. 🪖 #Braves #MLB

article.headline: Acuña Is on Fire and the Braves Are Running Away With the NL East

stories.title: Mariners Own AL West First Place After 7-Straight Wins

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Seattle Mariners:

📍 First place, AL West
📈 32-29 (above .500 for first time in months)
🔥 7-game winning streak
💥 3 walk-off wins in their last 4 games

Cole Young. Julio Rodriguez. This team is for real.

#Mariners #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The Seattle Mariners are in first place in the American League West. That sentence wasn't easy to write two months ago — but here we are.

After a seven-game winning streak that included three walk-off wins in four games, Seattle sits at 32-29 and owns a 2.5-game lead in the AL West. The team had been hovering around .500 for much of the season but has caught fire at exactly the right time. Cole Young delivered the latest clutch moment — a walk-off RBI single in the 10th inning to beat the Mets 3-2. Julio Rodriguez had the best month of his career in May with 10 home runs.

The AL West has no dominant team right now. The Rangers and Astros — two franchises that defined the division for years — are both below .500. The Mariners are walking into a vacuum.

Can they hold on? July will tell us a lot. But for now, Seattle is a team worth taking seriously.
facebook.image_caption: First place. AL West. 7-game winning streak. The Seattle Mariners are for real in 2026. 🌊 #Mariners #MLB

article.headline: The Seattle Mariners Are in First Place — and the AL West Has Never Been More Wide Open
